failure_reason: Cited block p035_c0004 raw text is truncated ('Off-board fare collection machines to be installed at') and does not contain the street locations, the project name 'First Avenue/Second Avenue SBS', or the SAS construction zone context. The connection between the treatment and the project relies on un-cited page-level headings. Evidence does not support the relation on its face.

Cited block p006_c0006 says only 'Over 57,000 weekday riders' — it does not mention M15, any bus route, or the corridor. The connection to route_m15-cb8-2010 is based on adjacency to the previous block (p006_c0005) rather than explicit textual evidence within the cited block. Co-occurrence is not a relation.

Cited block p006_c0010 says only '537,000 residents' — it does not mention the corridor, the quarter-mile scope, or First/Second Avenue. The connection relies on un-cited section headings ('Within a ¼ mile:' under 'About the Corridor'). Block text alone does not establish the relation.

Cited block p006_c0011 says only '78% of households do not own a car' — it does not mention the corridor, the quarter-mile scope, or any geographic identifier tying it to corridor_first-second-avenues-brt. The connection relies on un-cited page structure.

Cited block p006_c0012 says only '57% of residents commute by transit' — it does not mention the corridor, the quarter-mile scope, or any geographic identifier. The connection relies on un-cited page structure.

Service-variant mismatch: the subject route_m15-cb8-2010 has payload service_variant='local' and route_type='local', but the cited block p012_c0005 explicitly describes 'Sources of delay on M15 Limited' — the Limited variant. The metric record also carries route='M15 Limited'. A local route record cannot serve as the subject for a Limited-specific metric.
Same service-variant mismatch: route_m15-cb8-2010 is 'local' but the pie chart in p012_c0005 is explicitly titled 'Sources of delay on M15 Limited' and the metric is for M15 Limited, not M15 local.
Same service-variant mismatch: route_m15-cb8-2010 is 'local' but the pie chart in p012_c0005 is explicitly titled 'Sources of delay on M15 Limited' and the metric is for M15 Limited, not M15 local.
Same service-variant mismatch: route_m15-cb8-2010 is 'local' but the pie chart in p012_c0005 is explicitly titled 'Sources of delay on M15 Limited' and the metric is for M15 Limited, not M15 local.

Cited block p013_c0006 says '17% improvement in travel time 29% less time stopped at red lights' — it does not name '34th Street Enhanced Bus Priority' or any project. The connection relies on an un-cited page heading (p013_c0002). Additionally, project_34th-street-enhanced-bus-priority is sourced from different documents (2010_01_14_brt_1st2nd_cac3, better_buses), not from this source, and has status='study' while the metric reports implemented results, suggesting possible temporal/project-phase mismatch.

Same issues as rel-34th-travel-time: cited block p013_c0006 does not name the project '34th Street Enhanced Bus Priority', the connection relies on un-cited headings, and the project record is sourced from different documents with status='study' while the metric reports implemented results.

Cited block p014_c0006 does not mention 'Fordham Road' or 'Fordham Road Select Bus Service'. The raw text only lists generic metrics ('Travel Time: 20% reduction...') without identifying the project they belong to. The connection between the project and metric relies on an unverified heading (p014_c0002) that is not included in cited_blocks. Co-occurrence in a document under an unseen heading is not a demonstrated relation.

Same core defect as rel-fordham-running-time: cited block p014_c0006 does not mention 'Fordham Road' or 'Fordham Road Select Bus Service'. The raw text 'Ridership: 10% increase from October 2007 to October 2008' lacks any project or location identifier. Connection to the Fordham Road SBS project is asserted from an unverified heading structure, not from the block text itself.

Same core defect: cited block p014_c0006 does not mention 'Fordham Road' or 'Fordham Road Select Bus Service'. The raw text 'Customer Satisfaction: 98% satisfied or very satisfied' lacks project/location attribution. The connection depends entirely on a heading (p014_c0002) outside the cited_blocks.

Cited block p006_c0007 only contains 'Bus speeds have been declining' with no mention of First Avenue, Second Avenue, M15, or any corridor/route identifier. The rationale claims this appears in an 'About the Corridor' section for First/Second Avenue, but the block text itself provides no evidence linking this generic claim to the specific corridor.

Cited block p006_c0008 only contains 'Ridership has been decreasing' with no mention of First Avenue, Second Avenue, M15, or any corridor/route identifier. The generic statement cannot be linked to a specific corridor based on block text alone.

Cited block p019_c0001 is only the section heading 'First Avenue/Second Avenue SBS'. The actual design goals content (claimed to be in p019_c0002) is not in the cited_blocks. A heading alone does not demonstrate that the specific design goals listed in the claim record belong to this project — the evidence must show the goals themselves.

Cited block p001_c0003 states 'Community Board 8 Transportation Committee Thursday, February 25, 2010' but does not mention the First Avenue/Second Avenue SBS project or any project. The event is only linked to the project through implicit document context, not through the block's own text.
